description: Storing a connection string or client secret in App Service / Function app settings.
body: Failure mode: a team pastes a storage connection string or client secret into app settings, creating a long-lived credential that leaks via config exports or never rotates. Detection signals: app settings contain keys/connection strings, the secret appears in deployment logs or source, and the resource has no managed identity. Do not store data-plane secrets in configuration. Safer fallback: enable a managed identity, grant it the specific data-plane RBAC role, use DefaultAzureCredential in code, and keep any unavoidable secret in Key Vault referenced by the identity.
